---

title: "02_Run_Sims.qmd"

format: html

---



```{r}

#| echo: true

#| warning: false

#| message: false

rm(list=ls())

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(openxlsx)
library(purrr)
library(esqlabsR)

# # Set working directory to this script location when running interactively
# if (requireNamespace("rstudioapi", quietly = TRUE) && rstudioapi::isAvailable()) {
#   doc_path <- rstudioapi::getActiveDocumentContext()
#   if (nchar(doc_path) > 0) setwd(dirname(doc_path))
# }
setwd("C:/Users/krina/OneDrive/NAMs/APriori_PBPK_NAM/03_APriori/Scripts")

```



```{r}
myProjectConfiguration <- createProjectConfiguration("../ProjectConfiguration.xlsx")

# Create `ScenarioConfiguration` objects from excel files

scenarioConfigurations <- readScenarioConfigurationFromExcel(

  scenarioNames = NULL,

  projectConfiguration = myProjectConfiguration

)



# Create simulation scenarios defined in the excel files and apply the custom parameters

myScenarios <- createScenarios(

  scenarioConfigurations = scenarioConfigurations)



# Adjust simulation run options, if necessary.

# E.g. disable check for negative values if required

simulationRunOptions <- ospsuite::SimulationRunOptions$new()

simulationRunOptions$checkForNegativeValues <- FALSE

# Use all logical cores minus one so the OS stays responsive.
# Cap at 8 to avoid PK-Sim memory issues on very large machines.
simulationRunOptions$numberOfCores <- min(8L, max(1L, parallel::detectCores() - 1L))



# Run simulations

simulatedScenariosResults <- runScenarios(

  scenarios = myScenarios,

  simulationRunOptions = simulationRunOptions

)

# Save results and store the path to the results for later re-use

outputFolder <- saveScenarioResults(simulatedScenariosResults, myProjectConfiguration)

# Remove PKML files written by esqlabsR — only CSVs are needed downstream

pkml_files <- list.files(outputFolder, pattern = "\\.pkml$",
                         full.names = TRUE, recursive = TRUE)
if (length(pkml_files) > 0) {
  file.remove(pkml_files)
  message("Removed ", length(pkml_files), " PKML file(s) from results folder")
}

```
